This family comprises approximately 220 species of flowering plants, primarily found in the Mediterranean region. Calcitrapoides glomerata is a small shrub native to Australia, with a classification that categorizes it as a succulent plant. intro: Calcitrapoides glomerata, also known as Calcitrapa or Globe Mallow, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calcitrapoides glomerata include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Calcitrapoides glomerata is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to promote wound healing and skin health.
